content.full_text:
State of California Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

September 15, 1980

Stan Ulrich
California Law Revision Commission
4000 Middlefield Road, Room D-2
Palo Alto, CA 94306

Dear Stan:

This letter will confirm our telephone conversation of September 12, 1980. At its meeting of September 5, 1980, the Conflicts of Interest Committee of the Fair Political Practices Commission recommended to the full Commission that the Conflict of Interest Code of the Law Revision Commission be approved with the changes noted in the copy attached to my memorandum of August 26, 1980, with the following exception. They did not recommend to the Commission that the provision be included which required an annual update of the disclosure category before any work could begin on a topic. The Committee felt that this annual review was superfluous as the Political Reform Act requires all agencies to amend their Codes when change is necessitated by changed circumstances. See Government Code Section 87306. Therefore, if in the future the Law Revision Commission begins to consider a topic which could affect a type of interest which is not currently in Category 1, that Category must be amended by the Law Revision Commission within 90 days of the beginning of their consideration of the topic. They also asked me, however, to discuss with you whether Category 1 in the Code covered the kinds of entities which could be affected by the Law Revision Commission's topics over the course of the past few years, rather than simply the last year. In our conversation you confirmed that the topics generally stretched over several years and that the list as currently written already covers all the kinds of interests which could have been affected by your topics over the past few years. We therefore agreed that the list would not need to be expanded before the Code goes into effect.

Finally, my memorandum raised the question of whether or not the people who conduct studies on a topic under contract

Mr. Stan Ulrich  
September 15, 1980  
Page Two

should be designated in the Code as consultants. The Committee did not recommend such designation to the full Commission.

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to call me again.

Sincerely,

Sarah T. Cameron  
Deputy Chief for  
Conflicts of Interest  
Legal Division

STC:nrw

sections.question_synthetic: Should the California Law Revision Commission's Conflict of Interest Code require an annual update of disclosure categories before work can begin on topics, and should contractors conducting topic studies be designated as consultants?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC Conflicts of Interest Committee recommended approving the Code without requiring annual updates (as Government Code Section 87306 already mandates amendments when circumstances change), confirmed that Category 1 already covers interests affected by topics over several years, and declined to require consultant designations for contract study conductors.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a formal advice letter confirming prior conversation and Committee recommendations. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections exist, but the legal advice is embedded in the body text discussing three specific issues: annual update requirements, Category 1 coverage, and consultant designations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Should the California Law Revision Commission's Conflict of Interest Code require an annual update of disclosure categories before work can begin on topics, and should contractors conducting topic studies be designated as consultants?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC Conflicts of Interest Committee recommended approving the Code without requiring annual updates (as Government Code Section 87306 already mandates amendments when circumstances change), confirmed that Category 1 already covers interests affected by topics over several years, and declined to require consultant designations for contract study conductors.

Cameron Deputy Chief for Conflicts of Interest Legal Division STC:nrw
embedding.summary: FPPC's Deputy Chief for Conflicts of Interest confirms Committee recommendations regarding approval of the Law Revision Commission's Conflict of Interest Code, clarifying that annual disclosure category updates are unnecessary and that the current Category 1 list is sufficient.
